title: What is the taxi exam?
summary: A computer-based multiple-choice test administered by Traficom (Finnish Transport and Communications Agency).

items.label: Questions
items.value: 50 multiple-choice questions

items.label: Time
items.value: 60 minutes

items.label: Format
items.value: 4 answer options per question — only one is correct

items.label: Language
items.value: Finnish. No translation available during the exam.

items.label: Where
items.value: Traficom examination centres across Finland

items.label: Cost
items.value: ~€60–80 per attempt (check Traficom for current fee)

items.label: Retakes
items.value: You can retake if you fail — no mandatory waiting period

note: The exam tests your knowledge of passenger safety, traffic rules, customer service, and special passenger needs — not your driving ability.

title: How to get a taxi licence
summary: The taxi driver's licence (taksinkuljettajan ajolupa) is separate from a regular driving licence.

items.label: Step 1
items.value: Hold a valid driving licence (category B minimum)

items.label: Step 2
items.value: Pass the taxi driver knowledge exam at Traficom

items.label: Step 3
items.value: Pass a first-aid course (EA1 level)

items.label: Step 4
items.value: Background check — no serious criminal record

items.label: Step 5
items.value: Apply for the ajolupa at Traficom — valid for 5 years

items.label: Renewal
items.value: Renew every 5 years — re-exam may be required

note: You must complete all steps before you can legally carry paying passengers in Finland.

title: The 4 exam categories
summary: All 50 questions come from exactly these four official categories. Know them — the exam is organised around them.

categories.name: Passenger Help & Safety
categories.description: Seatbelt rules, safe boarding and alighting, door safety, driver duties before moving the vehicle.
keyRules: Driver must ensure all passengers are safely seated before driving
Driver must remind passengers to wear seatbelts
Driver must assist passengers getting in and out safely
Vehicle must not move until all doors are properly closed

categories.name: Special Passenger Needs
categories.description: Assisting wheelchair users, elderly passengers, children, and passengers with visual or hearing impairments.
keyRules: Driver must ask if help is needed — never assume
Wheelchair must be properly secured before driving
Child safety seats must be used for passengers under 135cm
Guide dogs and assistance animals must be accepted

categories.name: Customer Service
categories.description: Receipts, pricing transparency, passenger rights, professional conduct, handling complaints.
keyRules: Driver must provide a receipt on request — no exceptions
Price estimate must be given if asked before the trip
Driver must not discriminate against any passenger
Taxi meter must be switched on at the start of every trip

categories.name: Transport & Traffic Safety
categories.description: Traffic rules specific to taxi operation, speed limits, stopping and parking, vehicle roadworthiness.
keyRules: All traffic rules apply equally to taxi drivers — no exceptions
Driver must not use a phone while driving without hands-free
Taxi rank rules must be followed (first in, first out)
Vehicle must be in roadworthy condition before every shift

title: On exam day
summary: What to expect when you walk into the examination centre.

items.label: Bring
items.value: Valid photo ID (passport or Finnish ID card)

items.label: Arrive
items.value: 15 minutes early — late arrivals may not be admitted

items.label: Device
items.value: Traficom provides the computer — no own device needed

items.label: Allowed
items.value: Nothing on the desk. No notes, no phone, no dictionary.

items.label: Questions
items.value: Read every word carefully — Finnish is precise

items.label: Flagging
items.value: You can mark questions to review before final submission — but it's best to answer every question on the first sequential pass and only flag the ones you want to rethink

items.label: Results
items.value: You see your score immediately after submitting

note: If your Finnish is weak, use this app's clue-word method. Many questions can be answered by spotting key Finnish words even without full comprehension.

title: The clue-word method
summary: How this app teaches you to pass — even with limited Finnish.

items.label: Insight
items.value: Finnish exam answers follow predictable language patterns

items.label: Positive clues
items.value: Words like varmistaa, huolehtia, tiedustella signal the CORRECT answer

items.label: Negative clues
items.value: Words like ilman, ei tarvitse, vain/ainoastaan signal the WRONG answer

items.label: Absolute words
items.value: Never/always/only (koskaan/aina/vain) are almost always traps

items.label: Obligation verbs
items.value: Tulee/pitää/on velvollinen = driver duty = usually correct

items.label: Practice
items.value: Use 'Spot the Clue' mode to train your eye before the real exam

note: This method doesn't replace learning Finnish — but it gives you a systematic edge when you're unsure. Most exam errors happen from misreading, not ignorance.
